name: Fibonacci
description: Retracement model that waits for pullbacks into Fibonacci levels that overlap with structure and continuation confirmation.

context: Fibonacci anchored to a clear impulse swing.
Retracement zone aligned with structure or liquidity.
Directional bias matching the broader trend unless explicitly countertrend.
setup: Pullback reaching the 0.382, 0.5, or 0.618 retracement zone.
Retracement tempo slowing through smaller bodies or rejection wicks.
Fib zone overlapping prior structure, VWAP, or a session extreme.
trigger: Rejection candle closing out of the fib zone.
Higher-low or lower-high break confirming the turn.
Blind limit entry reserved for high-confluence zones only.
confirmation: Fib zone overlapping another level likely to attract response.
Retracement depth remaining inside the planned invalidation.
invalidation: Clean close beyond the fib zone and structure pivot.
Pullback transitioning into a full reversal with opposite impulse.
noTrade: Choppy range lacking a clear impulse leg.
Nearby structure limiting follow-through before the trade can develop.

stopLoss: Protective stop beyond the fib zone and swing pivot.
Stop hidden beyond the structure low or high that invalidates trend.
Exact fib number not used as the stop location.
takeProfit: First target at the prior swing high or low.
Second target at extension or measured-move projection.
Faster profit-taking when retracement depth is large and trend quality is weaker.
timeExit: Post-rejection expansion appearing within the next few candles.
Intraday-only setup closed before session handoff.
Hold period ending when the timing window closes.
earlyExit: Price accepting through the retracement zone after entry.
